title: Global preservation
text: Random triplet accuracy proportion triangle distances relative ordering retained. used Wang et al. (2021) measure global structure preservation. Triplets tied input-space distances excluded denominator define relative ordering. sampled input triplets define ordering, result NA_real_.

title: Global preservation
text: random_pair_distance_correlation() measures Pearson correlation equivalent input output distances, similar method used Becht co-workers.

title: Global preservation
text: random_pair_distance_emd() converts distances empirical distribution compares via Earth Mover’s Distance (1D Wasserstein), based method used Heiser Lau.

title: Label retrieval

ACM. http://pages.cs.wisc.edu/~jdavis/davisgoadrichcamera2.pdf Keilwagen, J., Grosse, ., & Grau, J. (2014). Area precision-recall curves weighted unweighted data. PloS One, 9(3), e92209. https://dx.doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0092209

title: Local preservation
text: local preservation use nearest neighbor preservation. Pass vector k get back preservation different numbers neighbors. larger datasets, nn_preservation() can use approximate nearest neighbors via rnndescent. nearest-neighbor matrices another approximate nearest neighbor package, e.g. RcppAnnoy, use nbr_pres_knn function:
code: iris_x <- as.matrix(iris[, -5]) pca_iris <- stats::prcomp(iris_x, retx = TRUE, rank. = 2)$x nn_preservation(iris_x, pca_iris, k = c(15, 30)) # A function to wrap the RcppAnnoy API to return a matrix of the nearest # neighbor indices find_nn <- function(X, k = 10, n_trees = 50, search_k = k * n_trees) {   nr <- nrow(X)   nc <- ncol(X)    ann <- methods::new(RcppAnnoy::AnnoyEuclidean, nc)   for (i in 1:nr) {     ann$addItem(i - 1, X[i, ])   }   ann$build(n_trees)    idx <- matrix(nrow = nr, ncol = k)   for (i in 1:nr) {     res <- ann$getNNsByItemList(i - 1, k, search_k, FALSE)     if (length(res$item) != k) {       stop("search_k/n_trees settings were unable to find ", k,            " neighbors for item ", i)     }     idx[i, ] <- res$item   }   idx + 1 }  kin <- find_nn(as.matrix(iris[, -5]), k = 5) kout <- find_nn(pca_iris, k = 5)  # This should give very similar results to using nbr_pres on the distance # matrices, subject to the approximations employed by Annoy nbr_pres_knn(kin, kout, k = 5)

Quality assessment dimensionality reduction: Rank-based criteria. Neurocomputing, 72(7), 1431-1443. https://dx.doi.org/10.1016/j.neucom.2008.12.017 Lee, J. ., Peluffo-Ordonez, D. H., & Verleysen, M. (2015). Multi-scale similarities stochastic neighbour embedding: Reducing dimensionality preserving local global structure. what: QUality Assessment of Dimensionality Reduction Algorithms
title: QUality Assessment of Dimensionality Reduction Algorithms
text: R Package evaluating success embeddings dimensionality reduction methods (e.g. Principal Component Analysis, Sammon Maps, t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding).

title: QUality Assessment of Dimensionality Reduction Algorithms
text: See NEWS.md release notes.

title: QUality Assessment of Dimensionality Reduction Algorithms
text: package provides two ways evaluate performance embedding. generic consider “neighborhood preservation”: Find k-nearest neighbors point input space. output space. Count overlap. requires calculating Euclidean distance matrix input output spaces, without assumptions type dimensionality reduction carried . package provides quality measures based around concept. Alternatively, sort labelling applied points, point can treated target retrieval procedure: Rank points distance target point. See highly ranked list points label found. Construct Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curve, something like (e.g. Precision-Recall curve) Calculate Area Curve (AUC). Average points. needs output distance matrix, requires sort labeling usually reserved data intended supervised classification. Quadra can also provide help , requires PRROC package installed.

title: QUality Assessment of Dimensionality Reduction Algorithms
text: quadra makes use C++ code must compiled. may carry extra steps able build package: Windows: install Rtools ensure C:\Rtools\bin path. macOS: using custom ~/.R/Makevars may cause linking errors. sort thing potential problem platforms seems bite Mac owners . R Mac OS X FAQ may helpful work can get away . safe side, advise building quadra without custom Makevars.

title: QUality Assessment of Dimensionality Reduction Algorithms
code: # Embed with first two scores from PCA pca_iris <- stats::prcomp(iris[, -5], retx = TRUE, rank. = 2)$x  # Random triplet accuracy: proportion of triangle distances where the relative # ordering is retained. Used by Wang et al (2021) to measure global structure # preservation random_triplet_accuracy(iris, pca_iris)  # For large datasets, you can set the number of threads to use # (pretend this is a big dataset) random_triplet_accuracy(iris, pca_iris, n_threads = 2)  # If you plan to carry out multiple comparisons with the same (high dimensional) # data, transpose once outside the function and set is_transposed = TRUE for a # slight speed-up tiris <- t(iris[, -5])  pca_iris2 <- t(pca_iris) pca_iris1 <- t(stats::prcomp(iris[, -5], retx = TRUE, rank. = 1)$x) pca_iris3 <- t(stats::prcomp(iris[, -5], retx = TRUE, rank. = 3)$x) random_triplet_accuracy(tiris, pca_iris2, n_threads = 2, is_transposed = TRUE) random_triplet_accuracy(tiris, pca_iris1, is_transposed = TRUE, n_threads = 2) random_triplet_accuracy(tiris, pca_iris3, is_transposed = TRUE, n_threads = 2)  # Other ways to measure global preservation:  # measure Pearson correlation between equivalent input and output distances # similar to the method used by Becht and co-workers random_pair_distance_correlation(tiris, pca_iris3, is_transposed = TRUE, n_threads = 2)  # convert distances to an empirical distribution and compare them via Earth # Mover's Distance (1D Wasserstein) based on the method used by Heiser and Lau random_pair_distance_emd(tiris, pca_iris2, is_transposed = TRUE, n_threads = 2)  # For local preservation use nearest neighbor preservation # Pass a vector to k to get back the preservation for different numbers of # neighbors nn_preservation(tiris, pca_iris2, k = c(15, 30), is_transposed = TRUE)  # slower methods not recommended for large datasets  din <- as.matrix(dist(iris[, -5])) dout <- as.matrix(dist(pca_iris))  # Preservation of the 5-nearest neighbors for each point: returns a vector nbr_pres(din, dout, k = 5)  # Area under the RNX curve. This is like a weighted average over neighborhood preservation for a range of k # with a bias towards smaller k: returns a single value rnx_auc(din, dout)  # Install the PRROC package install.packages("PRROC")  # ROC AUC using Species as the label: returns a list with one AUC per factor level # Also the average over all levels roc_auc(dout, iris$Species)  # Precision-Recall AUC which some people prefer over ROC pr_auc(dout, iris$Species)

title: Neighborhood Preservation Between Distance Matrices — nbr_pres
text: Calculates neighborhood preservation observation dataset, represented two distance matrices. first matrix "ground truth", second estimation approximation. neighborhood preservation calculated row element d[, j] taken distance observation j.

title: Neighborhood Preservation Between Distance Matrices — nbr_pres
Neighborhood Preservation Between Distance Matrices — nbr_pres
text: din Distance matrix. "ground truth" reference distances. dout Distance matrix. set distances compare reference distances. k size neighborhood, k number neighbors include neighborhood.

title: Neighborhood Preservation Between Distance Matrices — nbr_pres
text: Vector preservation values, one row distance matrix.

title: Neighborhood Preservation Between Distance Matrices — nbr_pres
text: neighborhood preservation can vary 0 (neighbors common) 1 (perfect preservation). However, random performance gives approximate value k / (n - 1), k size neighborhood n number observations items dataset. Self-neighbors diagonal excluded row neighborhood overlap calculated.

title: Neighborhood Preservation Between Distance Matrices — nbr_pres
text: efficient way calculate preservation want calculate value multiple values k. global measures preservation, see rnx_auc().

title: Neighborhood Preservation Between Nearest Neighbor Matrices — nbr_pres_knn
text: Calculates neighborhood preservation observation dataset, represented two matrices indices nearest neighbors. first matrix "ground truth", second estimation approximation. neighborhood preservation calculated row element d[, k] taken index kth nearest neighbor .

title: Neighborhood Preservation Between Nearest Neighbor Matrices — nbr_pres_knn
text: Approximate nearest neighbor methods, e.g. RcppAnnoy, can find k-nearest neighbors quite efficiently makes calculating preservation values larger datasets feasible. neighborhood preservation can vary 0 (neighbors common) 1 (perfect preservation). nearest-neighbor matrices exclude self-neighbors, random performance gives approximate value k / (n - 1), k size neighborhood n number observations items dataset.

title: Nearest Neighbor Preservation — nn_preservation
text: Calculates overlap two nearest neighbors graphs.

title: Nearest Neighbor Preservation — nn_preservation
code: nn_preservation(   Xin,   Xout,   k = 15,   nn_method_in = "nnd",   metric_in = "sqeuclidean",   nn_method_out = "brute",   metric_out = "sqeuclidean",   is_transposed = FALSE,   n_threads = 0,   verbose = FALSE,   ret_extra = FALSE,   nn_args_in = list(),   nn_args_out = list() )

title: Nearest Neighbor Preservation — nn_preservation
text: Xin input data (usually high-dimensional), matrix data frame one observation per row, is_transposed = TRUE, one observation per column. title: Nearest Neighbor Preservation — nn_preservation
text: measure local structure preservation, k-nearest neighbor graph input data, output data calculated. observation number neighbors common graph calculated mean value entire dataset reported. two methods calculating nearest neighbor graph: brute force approach involving calculating pairs distances approximate nearest neighbors approach using nearest neighbor descent method Dong co-workers (2011). brute force approach scales square number observations linear number features. nearest neighbor descent method likely economical high dimensional input. output data often low-dimensional (e.g. 2D), brute force neighbor search often feasible Xout even Xin. finer control nearest neighbor calculations, can pass extra arguments functions via nn_args_in nn_args_out lists. See documentation rnndescent::brute_force_knn() rnndescent::nnd_knn() functions details. nearest neighbor search can time-consuming, set ret_extra = TRUE, return value function list includes nearest neighbor graph Xin Xout. graph can passed Xin Xout re-used. useful comparing fixed Xin multiple Xout, e.g. different dimensionality reduction methods used, parameters output dimensionality random number seed modified.

title: Nearest Neighbor Preservation — nn_preservation

Graphs calculated function self-excluded. Older cached self-inclusive graphs detected stripped warning. dist (optional) matrix dimensions idx, containing equivalent distances. information actually used preservation function included output nearest neighbor calculation. provide nearest neighbor data, matrix need present.

Observations label Nth observation count positive observations. final reported result average observations. Rows undefined AUC values, rows define positive negative examples, excluded overall per-label averages. rows remain average, average NA_real_. what: quadra: QUality Assessment of Dimensionality Reduction Algorithms — quadra-package
title: quadra: QUality Assessment of Dimensionality Reduction Algorithms — quadra-package
text: Quantifies dimensionality-reduction embeddings global distance-preservation metrics, local nearest-neighbor preservation metrics, co-ranking metrics, label-based retrieval AUC metrics.

title: quadra: QUality Assessment of Dimensionality Reduction Algorithms — quadra-package
text: Maintainer: James Melville jlmelville@gmail.com Authors: James Melville jlmelville@gmail.com

title: Random Pair Distance Correlation — random_pair_distance_correlation
text: Evaluates preservation global structure dimensionality reduction results using Pearson correlation coefficient randomly selected distances, similar method Becht co-workers (2019).

Pearson correlation coefficient two sets distances reported. differs slightly procedure Becht paper randomly samples subset observations exhaustively calculates pair-wise distances within subset.

title: Random Pair Distance Correlation — random_pair_distance_correlation
text: Becht, E., McInnes, L., Healy, J., Dutertre, C. ., Kwok, . W., Ng, L. G., ... & Newell, E. W. (2019). Dimensionality reduction visualizing single-cell data using UMAP. Nature biotechnology, 37(1), 38-44.

title: Random Pair Distance Correlation — random_pair_distance_correlation
code: iris_pca2 <- stats::prcomp(iris[, -5], rank. = 2, scale = FALSE, retx = TRUE)$x random_pair_distance_correlation(iris, iris_pca2) #> [1] 0.9997105  # If you plan on comparing the results of multiple output methods, then # pre-transposing the input data can save time tiris <- t(iris[, -5]) iris_pca1 <- stats::prcomp(iris[, -5], rank. = 1, scale = FALSE, retx = TRUE)$x iris_pca3 <- stats::prcomp(iris[, -5], rank. = 3, scale = FALSE, retx = TRUE)$x random_pair_distance_correlation(tiris, t(iris_pca1), is_transposed = TRUE) #> [1] 0.9977412 random_pair_distance_correlation(tiris, t(iris_pca2), is_transposed = TRUE) #> [1] 0.9997525 random_pair_distance_correlation(tiris, t(iris_pca3), is_transposed = TRUE) #> [1] 0.9999674

what: Area Under the RNX Curve — rnx_auc
title: Area Under the RNX Curve — rnx_auc
text: RNX curve formed calculating rnx_crm metric different sizes neighborhood. value RNX scaled according natural log neighborhood size, give higher weight smaller neighborhoods. AUC 1 indicates perfect neighborhood preservation, AUC 0 due random results. Self-neighbors distance-matrix diagonal excluded co-ranking matrix calculated.

title: quadra 0.2.0 (July 3 2026)
text: Many small fixes corner cases improved validation. rnndescent now CRAN, longer need install github. grain_size thread-handling removed API exposed . Fix issue index-nearest neighbor graphs allowed metrics didn’t need distances. nn_preservation() now excludes “self”-neighbor (.e. item ) nearest-neighbor graphs. calculating graph internally, k + 1 extra neighbors requested rnndescent.

title: quadra 0.1.0 (November 20 2023)
text: New function: random_triplet_accuracy, used PaCMAP paper evaluating global preservation. New function: nn_preservation nearest neighbor preservation function can use approximate nearest neighbors multiple threads faster calculations. also supports metrics Euclidean. New function: random_pair_distance_correlation evaluating global preservation, similar method used Becht co-workers. New function: random_pair_distance_emd evaluating global preservation, similar method Heiser Lau. Changed license GPL3+. Building package now requires compiling C++. package also now non-CRAN dependency: rnndescent, github.

title: quadra 0.1.0 (November 20 2023)
text: Added NEWS.md file track changes package.

title: quadra 0.0.0.9000 (December 31 2021)
text: Initial version. MIT Licensed.
